His support staff were excellent as well, and their pricing was very fair.

We called on a Thursday for a quote and by Saturday morning they were already working on the roof. The quality of service was so impressive that we also had them install skylights and gutters, which turned out great.

[{"Name":"Don Lacey","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX0heuzdaXJ4f-YHR4yKQ-p5DkC5Uq5ArlwH2jdUebaqteroKzW=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We were planning to add a roof over an existing deck and also replace the original roof (shingles) as part of the same project. After obtaining 3 quotes we decided to go with Clarke for a few reasons - Graham (owner) was exceptional to work with throughout the whole process by suggesting shingle colors and styles, covering all aspects of what the job entailed, ensuring the challenging parts of the roof (flat valleys) were properly installed, helped us with the skylight purchase/install, was open and fair on the pricing and able to meet our timeline. They worked very well with our construction team to install new roofing right after that build to ensure we didn't have any rain risk. The crew was very competent, professional and friendly - putting in a few tough days in high temps to get it finished. Overall the job was wrapped in a few days, much shorter than I anticipated (and also included a garden shed). The end result is an upgrade that looks great and adds long term value to our home. Clarke Roofing is highly recommended!
Per the pix we went with CertainTeed Landmark shingles (Weather wood?) with a high profile ridge cap.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICp_NDjVQ&fid=0x0:0x645cbd9bdd8e27ba"],"When":"2023-7-30"},{"Name":"High Mark Kamloops","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL1kKCZg9Z1QmCf-NIqf3f3vJ0orCyay7Y89Rk80aMWKAkk-w=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We had a commercial roof complete replacement done by Clarke Roofing and really couldn’t be happier.

The finished product looks wonderful!
If ever in need of a roofing company I would highly recommend Clarke roofing.
Ruth L","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Richard Tennant","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocL5QFOfgf_NSI6PYI7T30S-6oanggEv0n8KePcYdI3fKH4dzw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"When planning our shingle replacement, Graham made a site visit promptly and scheduled the project to match our timeline. Each crew member was courteous and professional.  They did a meticulous cleanup at the end of it all.  We are pleased with the end result and would most certainly use Clarke Roofing for future roofing needs.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Andrea Lemieux","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLFRtb1ldDq9dOeRgSILW6K9_Tu0V9t67wXGLyhrATuPY2_iw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We had our roof replaced last month. Graham from Clarke Roofing Services was very courteous and professional and provided detailed information regarding all aspects of the materials that would be used and how the work would be done. Adam and Paul did a beautiful job and they were a pleasure to have around for a few days. They brightened up the hazy environment for sure. We would highly recommend Clarke Roofing Services  and we are confident that the roof is now leak free and will last for many years to come. - Debra and Eugene","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Brett Burgeson","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUQK38hAHxuYkFekj1QvLkU8lTLtg_iFKHtP16Xio_MClu66LYR=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Graham M is a great owner and roofer! Go see them to get great work!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Linda R","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJPRRZgyBnkNMC30IcrEZ8FxC76dxhxdNb-yUWS5ggfwWD19A=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"From beginning to end, my experience with this company has been excellent. The owner is knowledgeable and professional and tries to provide the best price available to his customers. His crew is polite and efficient and were able to install my new roof in two days. I am more than pleased with the work done by Clarke Roofing Services and I would highly recommend this company to anyone who is looking for an honest, quality company to deal with.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"David & Shelley Hess","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWL0NZjydZxDsVUqrmYyDemT6pZZVj0HMOvY4OMgTIUy-_zuRY=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"June 12-15, 2023 Clarke Roofing Services Ltd.  removed an old, leaking tar and gravel roof and replaced with new torch on roofing, reusing the existing flashing at our request.

From our very first communication, absolutely every detail our experience with Graham, his crew including Cory, Paul, Adam and Michael, and the work they did, has been exceptionally excellent, down to absolutely every single detail.

Our phone message requesting a quote was promptly answered within a day.  Even as we initially spoke, Graham immediately had our house on Google Earth for clearest understanding and a visual of our request.  His skills and experience enabled him to give us a very accurate rough quote, and the option to continue with his traveling to make a firm quote.

The quote being more than reasonable (we received three quotes from four we contacted), Graham promptly traveled from Kamloops to 100 Mile House and in person confirmed that quote, no surprises to either party.

The work was booked promptly in an extremely timely manner, for which we remain EXTREMELY grateful!

Due at 8 a.m. on a Monday the men were here ten minutes early (a very encouraging note) and were on the roof at work by 8.

We have never worked with more polite respectful, thoughtful and diligent men, all five.  Graham and his team set an AWESOME tone and example which is a joy to behold!!

Weather being a factor, the men willingly started early, day two and three to ensure all was sealed before forecasted rain came.  Their attention to every detail is astounding and outstanding!

The work site was kept impeccably clean.

We had two sun tunnels, uncommon in such a roof and requiring special consideration and thought to achieve most desirable outcomes.  Graham gladly worked with us to that end.

The roof was completed well ahead of time, even with weather challenges, and we were sorry to see these men gone!!

Every single detail of our experience with Graham and Clarke Roofing Services Ltd has been so far better than words will convey.  They are a busy, successful company for all the right reasons.

As their expertise covers such a wide range of needs, and their integrity, workmanship, diligence, and courtesy is beyond excellent, we are honored to highly recommend them!!

It was both our supreme privilege and honor to contract Clarke Roofing Services Ltd. to meet our needs!","Images":null,"When":""}]

We wanted our ensuite renovated and this included extending our small shower stall into a full sized shower with a wide bench. We also wanted the ventilation system improved. Bryan, owner and Nathan, project manager met us at our home and after listening to our requests and some poking around, Bryan reassured us that by totally gutting the ensuite we could be able to get a larger shower area.  When Joe, the designer came to take measurements, we decided to include the guest bathroom in our renovation at the same time. Joe was very attentive to our requests and designed our dream ensuite that included a large shower space as well as closing off our closet entrance from the ensuite and creating a new entrance to our closet from the bedroom. However, Joe decided to move to the lower mainland on short notice and we were assigned to Amanda. We were impressed at how smoothly we were able to transition to working with Amanda. Amanda was attentive to our requests and worked closely with us to help pick out our products and to ensure that we were within the budget.
The Elite team were consistently respectful, professional, clean and reliable and the sub contractors they hired were similar. Nathan attended daily during demolition then weekly / as needed and Brian and Amanda also stopped by from time to time. This reassured us that the work was being monitored. Although there was some delay, work was completed in a timely fashion and our ensuite exceeded our expectations. We are very happy with our renovations and like the fact that is company also deals with maintenance. We will definitely refer Interior Elite to our friends and family.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC3ruaTUA&fid=0x0:0x64dfe50b6311440e"],"When":"2024-11-5"},{"Name":"Carl Cantelon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUNoezjoeBKEQAwMFgTdkveGVC4l_kicCNhAnrsp1RB-cYizkAQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Nathan, a project manager from Elite, was excellent. He offered clear, practical guidance on my basement renovation and had his team repair a foundation crack quickly and professionally. Highly recommended!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kimberly Hall","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX3JvGUJWB68M0s-IN6rruPfrpEwBIEShuHKTkp0snZDYdtqKc=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Interior Elite Contracting Limited
​After much deliberation, we decided that we should complete some renovations of our retirement home.  After doing our research and reading about Elite, we arranged a meeting with them.
​The process involved a very skillful gal with an eye for detail in Amanda.  We told her what we wanted and after some back and forth and suggestions agreed on the renovation plan.
​The Interior Elite team came on board with Victor and Dustin weighing in.  On January 9th the process began and was completed in March.
​Communication and inspections were regular and we knew who would be coming and going from day to day and week to week with a provided calendar.
​They turned our ensuite into a spa, main bath into a wonderful guest escape and the kitchen into a very functional piece of beauty.  The kitchen was gutted and everything was replaced with a new modern look with white and blue cabinetry, quartz counter tops and back splash.  The storage added was a welcome addition to our home.
​The attention to detail and meticulous way they progressed were remarkable.  Throughout it all we remained in our home and the messes were small and mostly cleaned up in short order.
​The trades that they brought in to attend to electrical, plumbing, drywalling, painting, flooring, cabinetry, and glass work were amazing.  The finished product is a testament to the ability of Elite to multi task and create an atmosphere of teamwork, obvious throughout the project.
​We are ready to enjoy our home long into our retirement and a source of pride for both of us.
Kimberly Hall & Mark Doratti
Kamloops, BC","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICxncyE4gE&fid=0x0:0x64dfe50b6311440e"],"When":"2023-5-23"},{"Name":"Elizabeth Lamar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLziv81Er7Mjv4PJjVUKsgzawcELbuCliCQMfDgyBObLGuezQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Elite Contracting recently completed a renovation that included new flooring, new baseboards, trim and  painting the complete interior of my home.  Working with Devin made this project very easy.  He was always quick to respond to any inquiries and many times came over to see how things were going. The subs and trades people were professional, friendly, courteous, and their work was  of top quality!  Devin kept the project on time and on budget.  I am extremely happy with the results and of Elite's performance.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Marguerite","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUTST3r_0uj_6LpzZdFzXGB2HjDSc72RHzSoefYLvtteWmqit7m=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I was fortunate enough to find this company of talented professionals to add a second floor addition. Where I first noticed its calibre was in the quality of communication that was maintained throughout the early stage of quote and blue prints. They worked with my draftsman, the city ref: permit and kept me informed of any possible hiccups we may encounter given I live on the river and must maintain the integrity of the riparian area while building up. So five stars for clear, timely communication.

The next thing I noticed was the project manager made regular visits to the site. This gave us the confidence that Elite kept its finger on the pulse of the project and knew exactly what was happening with the project and the subcontractors every step of the way.

Elite's subcontractors were respectfully and friendly. They were never intrusive and always considered us as homeowners living on site while the work was being done.

The work was finished in a timely manner. The quality of work was beyond our expectations. Once completed I called the project manager on one or two minor items which were addressed without question or cost.

And speaking of cost Elite stayed on budget and during a time when materials and lumber was in constant fluctuation.

This year (2023) we are having Elite back to work on yet another project.

I can without any hesitation recommend this company for anything and all things to do with construction.

They are a five star company.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICh4oOaLQ&fid=0x0:0x64dfe50b6311440e"],"When":"2023-2-9"},{"Name":"Tanner Sherwood","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWCGId96BvuRGHfz9LxtmjM10LUh5FzX3Ef4ANUbe0aUoYXhWpS=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Bryan and the team at Elite are a great contractor to work with. We've worked with them as their closet provider on many projects, and we can say that contractors that make a point to include upgraded closets as standard in their builds take that same care and attention to the things you can't see.

They've got a very qualified team, and their project coordinators keep tight schedules and good communication which makes all the difference working with them.

We recommend Elite to tackle either new home construction or renovations. [{"Name":"Rochelle Desjardins","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWJ7A_Cvl6RN5RWD6Rq3CgHnD1FqWYUSgUISxfDyKEB_ffQjjgKJA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We just had our gutters completely replaced and they look amazing. High quality gutters and professional installers!! Great price!!
Thanks so much for your hard work Jadon and Ross!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Graeme Ward","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLaZbwts_rUxt70YbmAWZMx7eh39lmY2D_KITC9_fkOPTaNCg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Of all the contractors I've hired ArmourGrade was by far the worst. Every time I walk outside I seem to find another problem. We had them replace gutters and soffit, here are just some of problems:

- Gutter leaks (they've attempted to fix one leak 3 times, after their third attempt I just fixed it myself).

- They don't know how to merge two pieces of j-channel together. Every joint around our entire house and garage is just two pieces overlapped, it looks absolutely horrible.

- Putting up soffit in a straight line over a 25 ft span? Not these guys. I cringe every time I look down the length of my garage.

- There are holes and gaps all over the place where animals can get into our roof.

- Water is getting down behind the aluminum facia they installed. During the last storm it was actually shooting a 4 ft stream of water out of the corner of the facia straight onto our deck. I've been told that shingle overhang was out of the scope of work. Here's my favourite bullet point from ArmourGrade's brochure when they sold me on the job: Inspect and ensure sufficient shingle overhang and drip edge.

- They installed a single piece of drip edge without asking me. The workers on site told me that they did it because they didn't think the shingles overhung the gutters enough. Right, like they are supposed to do (see above). A few days later they tacked on $168 to my final bill for it. A 10ft piece of drip edge costs less than $14. All they did was shove it under the shingles and walk away. In later discussion, I was told they actually installed 28 ft of drip edge. Their final invoice included drip edge installation, unit count 1, $168. I found the first 10ft of that 28ft long piece of drip edge, still not sure where the other 18ft went.

- Update, they came back weeks ago to fix more leaks, and put more drip edge up under the roof edge. They used MY drip edge, that I paid for. They actually said about the product I bought for a 15thof what they charged me "oh, this is better than what we use". Hilariously, they also said the drip edge that had previously been installed was not done properly. They were two of the same guys who had done the initial work. Said they'd come back to fix facia that overlaps the wrong way... it still overlaps the wrong way.

- When putting in a vent for a bathroom fan in the soffit they completely shredded the ducting on roofing nails. I didn't bother calling them about this one, I had to pull my fan out of the ceiling and rerun all the venting myself with the soffit already installed.

- I asked them to install black gutters and for all the drain tile caps at the bottom of the downspouts they installed white caps spray painted black on site. I asked them to get me black caps and they said this is what they always do and that they couldn't get black caps. I told them the paint would just come off and sure enough in a few months I had speckled white and black caps. I contacted their manager who actually told me that I should use better paint... what? This is a gutter company that can't get black drain caps, claims spray paint on plastic is an industry standard and when called out for it tells the customer to paint them again but with better paint than they used.

Anyone who has given ArmourGrade 5 stars needs to go outside and actually look at their workmanship.

I asked for a partial refund including compensation for the time I had to spend fixing their mistakes at the same rate I paid for additional labour on the job (either $80 or $100/hr depending on which employee you ask), and was offered $50/hr. When I said this was pretty insulting I was told that $50/hr is what they would pay a sub contractor, and aligns with industry standards. Should we do the math?

Hypothetically, let's say ArmourGrade sends someone to do 4 hrs of work on my house at $80/hr, and then I have to spend 2 hrs fixing their work and get reimbursed based on their ‘industry standard’:

-	The labourer makes $200
-	ArmourGrade makes $20 ($120 profit - $100 back to me)
-	I pay $220 and waste two hours of my time ($320 - $100)

Huh?","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC7-Ie79AE&fid=0x0:0xa36e2532d2fb2615"],"When":"2024-8-12"},{"Name":"Noel Gopsill","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKBiDQr-uG2607PW5rD-NQdd3qWEpan10O-HRd-mThXie4JaQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A very good experience with this company, the crew started at 7am and worked until 9:30pm to get the job done. It was a 40+ deg day and I was surprised they never quit working. A good job done and a great facelift for our house.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgICe0Ma0Ug&fid=0x0:0xa36e2532d2fb2615"],"When":"2022-9-3"},{"Name":"Shawn Crawford","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJUFjtZc0_GXUQSWHKJwPxN41783kGQoSH3rDUfqcj4W3a4Bg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The crew showed up on time, were very professional. started work right away. address: 1764 Kelly Douglas Rd Unit A, Kamloops, BC V2C 5S4

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Amber Glennie","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUGn1tztP_IZAIKYtHMfeOew6FWMdv1P5SeAiolRwrKo63LARIWsQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"If I could give negative stars I would. In January 2021, our basement flooded due to a broken water main. We were recommended On-side restoration for the repairs, It is now August of 2023 and we are still dealing with this nightmare of a company. From the beginning there were missed appointment dates, months with zero contact or updates, and a multitude of lies and blame shifting. Devin was our project manager, and let me tell you the review below calling him "the worst" is no exaggeration. I think the fact he has been let go from the company is clear proof of his inability. After multiple attempts to communicate with Devin, we finally had to get in contact with his manager Andrew Scott. We arranged a meeting to sit down with both Devin and Andrew and go over some of the issues we were having.

During this meeting Andrew explained that there were some staffing issues and that  Devin was not getting the support from the company that he needed. We made a list of the things that needed to be addressed: The vanity that was damaged by OnSides technicians needed to be replaced, a wall had to be repainted, and the new floors were lifting behind the toilet .  We discussed at length with them the frustrations we were feeling with lack of communication and the time it was taking them to fix three rooms. We were ensured by Andrew that they would be taken care of, and moving forward we would have regular communication.

Fast forward a few months, I think about October, the vanity that was damaged by Onsides technicians was finally being replaced.They hired a third party to install the new vanity , and when transporting the vanity into the house, they did significant damage to a vehicle in the garage. Now to give some context about this vehicle, it had never been in the garage. The car arrived at the house in July after having the engine rebuilt and a whole bunch of other work done. From July to September the car was in the driveway, we had to bring the car into the garage because winter was right around the corner, when they finally arrived in October to fix the vanity that they broke, they scratched the side of the drivers fender and the drivers door.

We were told the damage would be assessed and there would be compensation for said damage. One day we received a letter in the mail asking for payment of the deductible. As far as we are concerned the claim is not finished as the damage to the car has not been assessed. We voiced this fact to both our insurance company and the restoration company, and are told to get a hold of the territory Manager (Jeff) I contact him, explain the story for what feels like the 100th time and am advised he will personally look at the damage to the car, the first week of August. It is now August 17, are you surprised we haven't heard from him ? So almost two years later we are left with VERY POOR QUALITY repairs and a damaged vehicle. On side refused to sign any sort of document claiming fault for the vehicle, after verbally agreeing they would. They are now claiming the car is in too poor condition to notice new damage, even though Devin came to our house and photographed said damage. Instead they have threatened to send the owed deductible to a collection agency, which will in turn damage our credit score.

Some other losses that we took include 12 months of rent that we lost on our basement suite ($9600), two days of wages lost waiting for a water tech and cleaning tech that stood us up ($336), our laundry machines were left disconnected for almost 5 months and we had to do dry cleaning for that entire time ($1200) and now our scratched car ($1500-2000). So in all we are at about a $12,000-$13,000 loss. Thanks On-side!

0 out of 10, would not and will never recommend to anyone, ever.

Here are some pictures of the car they claim in poor condition and the workmanship they claim as "quality"","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDpnI-EWA&fid=0x0:0x71202b43d3f99930"],"When":"2023-8-17"},{"Name":"Sahali Coop","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJKL90pffLPCLyMDiw9teOsJJOdeG8mlQ5YpeRjsRM9Dq240Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"On Side Restoration Services was engaged to address flood damage in one of the units at our complex, caused by a main water line break during the winter months. Their team provided a thorough and detailed evaluation, along with a clear and accurate estimate for the required repairs.

The repair process was completed efficiently and within the projected timeline. Throughout the project, On Side Restoration maintained a high level of professionalism, ensuring consistent communication with both the client and property management. The quality of their work was excellent, and we were very satisfied with the overall experience.

We would confidently recommend On Side Restoration Services for future work.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"RWK","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKs8Nnl3C_gQGp6pHRq2EBZH48TrqyT4U5FEd6cf8D8n3LTwg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I had a flood in my basement and Onside promptly responded once I reached out. I returned a bundle of shingles to Convoy on October 1, 2020.  At that time I was told that my refund would come in the form of a cheque from their head office, since I had paid for the original order online due to Covid.  I waited for six weeks before I called the store in Kamloops.  The fellow I talked at that time told me he would look into it and get back to me.  I waited for another two weeks.  Called the store again.  Spoke to another person, who did call me back after investigating and said I'd have a cheque within a week.  A month later I called and asked for the head office number so I could call myself and find out what the problem was. Was told that they didn't give out that information (and the head office phone number isn't available online, I checked).  Anyway, the story goes on and on. It was a very stressful situation, but the team from First General handled everything incredibly well.

Eric, the manager, was excellent at coordinating the schedule and keeping communication clear the entire time. Robertson quickly removed all the water and handled the drying process efficiently. After that, the team removed the damaged drywall and contaminated items without delay.

Guillermo and another team member later came to finish the cleanup and left everything looking great.

Unfortunately, this is the third flood we’ve experienced, and out of all the companies we’ve worked with, First General has been the most organized, efficient, and professional. I would absolutely recommend them.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kaylee Meeres","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLTOJgasn3kkSvdVDTPth8XNqlD0yTpfsD0uMOeU1iIk3mmVA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"First General completed a lot of work after we experienced a flood that impacted both our up and downstairs. They managed the project from start to finish, completed restoration work, asbestos abatement and moved furniture and cleaned. Erik and his whole team were very communicative in explaining the work they were doing, the processes and the timelines. They showed my partner the containment and safety measures they took during the abatement process, and followed WorkSafe BC procedures. I would recommend them to anyone.
I would also recommend the contractor, Andrew, who was subcontracted to rebuild the walls, ceiling and baseboards. Highly recommend for anyone looking for reliable, high-quality construction work.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgMCo4vjzpQE&fid=0x0:0x9ee76f0f73a3c455"],"When":"2025-4-15"},{"Name":"keerti kumar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWNoL70QXkuO8BuUnkhg_FZeSLVin_AdfxHoTslyyR--rS3s2L9=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I had RenoHills Construction renovate my bathroom, and they did an amazing job! They installed a new bathtub, tiled the walls and floors, and handled all the plumbing and other details. The team was always on time, polite, and kept my home clean during the work. They were very patient with my questions and made sure I was happy with every step.

The quality of their work is excellent, and my bathroom looks fantastic now! The tiles are perfectly placed, and the finishing touches show their attention to detail. They completed the project on time and within the budget we discussed. I’m really impressed with how everything turned out and would definitely recommend RenoHills Construction for any home renovations. They made the whole process stress-free and delivered exactly what I wanted!","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIDnl6bI1gE&fid=0x0:0x9ee76f0f73a3c455"],"When":"2024-10-11"},{"Name":"Prajwal Bhola","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIzLd2uc2TShAlKEXAHvuNTNrtiHH2hns1Ibn1vj2qDUdtyJw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"As the renter of the unit being renovated, I feel it’s important to share the extremely unprofessional and stressful experience I went through with this contractor and their team. Key issues:

No formal contract provided, despite multiple follow-ups. Everything was vague and verbal.

Promised to complete the work in 3 days, but it dragged on for 7 days, with no valid reason.

The contractor was fully aware the landlord is pregnant, yet refused to move any items. I had to do all the heavy lifting and clean-up myself.

Poor scheduling – they worked for 3 full days and for 4days after 4 PM, disturbing other residents and getting us into trouble with building management.

No proper plan or organization  requests like finishing the bedroom first were completely ignored.

They outsourced the work to unbriefed workers who didn’t know what to do and kept asking me for instructions.

One of those hired workers, Yuvraj Singh, even called and abused me on the phone, which is completely unacceptable.

Varry Bhinder, who was supposedly in charge, acted extremely rude and dismissive. Even after I explained the landlord had full permission for the work, he kept making excuses to avoid doing his part.

They painted kitchen cabinets with a brush instead of a spray, leaving visible streaks and marks. The paint is already peeling off.

Midway through the job, they changed the price and scope, especially for areas like the equipment room, despite earlier agreements.

We were never told about additional charges for a proper finish—they only mentioned it after the work was done.

They worked late into the night without warning, causing noise complaints from neighbours, and we even received a fine.

Despite knowing we live in the unit, they constantly complained about furniture and belongings, which they were fully aware of before accepting the job.

Communication was terrible, there was no transparency, and their only goal seemed to be finishing fast—not doing it right or keeping the client satisfied.

This experience caused unnecessary physical and emotional stress, especially given my landlord’s pregnancy and our attempts to cooperate throughout. I cannot recommend this contractor to anyone expecting professionalism, respect, or quality work.

Wish there is an option 0.1 star rating and I will choose that....","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"eljhay sadural","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUQ2uZz1Use2cQ3qIb8zinY4QFH1X5r2w04D1goPPL8M3Nye58=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Very Poor experience with Renohills Construction base on my experience to them. They have no transparency from start to finish when you see these pictures and videos I gathered their work is not good. they don't have a ONE WORD and their approach is not good they know I am pregnant and above all their work is not professional. just painted even if it is peeling their work. I thought they were professional because they already have a company and I know they studied it, but they are not. they said they would finish my house in within 3 days but it took 7 days, this company is not recommended they are not professional of there work and they have no heart to help their clients, only concern is to do the job poorly. They don't care if their work is done right, they left your house a disaster because the painting wasn't done well. Then while you're expressing your opinion, the owner gets angry and raising his voice, no respect even though I'm pregnant. Client not satisfied on their work.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Parvinder Kaur","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocILF6an-xTsSFsSTKsAlRjbiWqjijpAM4ZoFEJURgo_KZU5ha8=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Renohills construction completed my office remodelling project on time and with great attention to detail. I've had excellent interactions/experieces with a few of the staff.

Aaron has helped me out a few times now in a variety of ways. Also, management there have gone out of their way to assist me. The Pro desk takes treating Pro members with great care very seriously, always seeking to ensure we're satisfied.

I'm totally sold on Home Depot and wouldn't consider going anywhere elses. I've been a Pro member since opening my company and I'm not sure I recall a time when I've not been satisfied with their service/care of customers.

Great store, great staff, great deals, and if you're a customer, you can always expect to be treated warmly, kindly, and with appreciation.

Jast wanna add one other person who has definitely impressed me with their strong customer relations skills. Kim, who handles the Customer Service area often.
She's been fantastic, ways willing to help and go the extra mile.

Debra is great too, lol.

I've also had a couple great interactions with Deepak and would like to commend him as well for the fantastic customer service.

Daniel, Trusted Reno","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"T M","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVLjiQWi-5aL9ezAh8TLNiWDeMg2CdRMmaFTwoyQ3QepsqT_IJt=s120-c-rp-mo-ba3-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"I’ve been coming here for years, I just find it amazing how much the staff will avoid having to anything surrounding customer service or giving somebody a hand, When you show up, they have all the stuff you need. What I really appreciated was the in-person experience. It was refreshing to deal with a real person who took the time to come to my home, walk through everything face to face, and answer questions without rushing or pushing a sale. It was simply straightforward, respectful communication.

I ultimately decided to postpone the work for a year or two, and they were completely understanding and professional about it. That level of candor and respect really stood out. They did a good job but the only two issues I had: 1) Communication leading up to the appointment. I didn't receive a confirmation so I really wasn't sure if they were coming. So, I reached out to confirm. 2) The gutter on one side of my house was not cleaned. How can I tell? There was a plant growing that was there before and after the gutter cleaning. However, I called them and they came to clean it then took pictures to show me the evidence of the cleaning. What I liked is 1) their presentation and 2) the photos they shared with me on the job after it was done. 3) They also did some asphalt repairs and 4) provided additional recommendation for extending the lifetime of the asphalt. Overall, very good and the most competitive per square inch price for roof cleaning.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIABIhCxrevL3cFvh4aP3B8Q3Tdr&fid=0x0:0x45646945a4fb5a26"],"When":"2025-10-15"},{"Name":"Cayla Stiles-Clark","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKvhd8dT-j3vDJygLh9Cndn3MuaOQLuFzW7d8ovI7fcoEV8vYB=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Came across OK Roof Revival at a local home show in February after experiencing a roof leak (that got worse shortly after the initial interaction). They then came to my house a few weeks later and did a drone over with photos. They presented me with a detail account of my roof's condition and gave me affordable options.

It did then take several weeks for someone to come to the island to do the in person inspection, but that was very reasonable. They were able to schedule my home shortly afterwards for the repair and rejuvenation.

My only issues were with communications and issues with the financing after the repair. After the initial presentation, I kept having to follow up to get a set answer.

And then communications broke down when they had to reschedule the repair (was supposed to a Thursday and rescheduled to Sunday afternoon) I was ok with the reschedule after I rearranged my schedule for the Sunday. Then I received multiple appointment emails the day before (Saturday) kept changing the time on Sunday. While this was going on, a worker showed up on the Saturday to stage the day before the repair was scheduled and we were not home. We really didn't know what was going on. A call later, from Cameron, resolved the confusion.

The repair itself was well done as well as the rejuvenation. They did a beautiful job on my roof. It looks fantastic. They supplied before, during and after pictures.

It did take a few days to get the final invoice cleared up as the wrong financing was set up but it was fixed by their office within a week of the completion of the repair.

All in all, it was a good experience and I would still recommend them over any local roofing company. (and I did have 2 other roofers look at my roof who were useless).

Thank you OK Roof Revival for all that you have done.","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEKOr9riNncPcDA&fid=0x0:0x45646945a4fb5a26"],"When":"2025-6-3"},{"Name":"Wes Winger","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI95cV-vMjfVwkB1PwapDfOjHHnakKsYX_LrO8asM75a2fM2A=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Had a great initial meeting/consultation with the owner. Had all sorts of trouble getting the warranty document (more disclaimers than warrantable items).  Clean up was minimal but the biggest complaint was dealing with the Sales Manager Dan M.  Rude and pompous!!!   If you  contract with them, make sure you take your own before and after pictures!!!!  Cameron (the owner) smoothed things over.  I would deal with Cameron again!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"P Hewko","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJZ-UHhSNZ5NjBK6SzQjmUThyiZAISXWDNzw_yMKBx9PMgilw=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Found a leak sunday night, called OK Roof Revival monday morning, they came out same day, flew a drone over my house, assesed the issue, came out tuesday with materials and made the necessary repairs. user_reviews: [{"Name":"Chris Klohn","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKyp7wq_4vfwlJhPVMsYX77Q3filVq4OfgdpQ-mpp445j847g=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I reached out to Pristine Pressure Washing Services after we completed an inground pool excavation in November. Five days of running a dump truck with a pup, excavator and skidsteer left our driveway, road and side entry looking like a war zone.  The crew showed up on time with two 3200 PSI pressures washers, jerry cans full of gas, additional hose, and a backup unit should one of them fail.  They proceeded to run the machine for 5.5 hours straight , transforming the side of the house, driveway and road back into it's original state.  Prior to pressure washing, the crew needed to shovel out a mixture of frozen clay, mud, ice and snow that was packed up against our fence and on the driveway. [{"Name":"Rochelle Desjardins","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWJ7A_Cvl6RN5RWD6Rq3CgHnD1FqWYUSgUISxfDyKEB_ffQjjgKJA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We just had our gutters completely replaced and they look amazing. High quality gutters and professional installers!! Great price!!
Thanks so much for your hard work Jadon and Ross!!","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Graeme Ward","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocLaZbwts_rUxt70YbmAWZMx7eh39lmY2D_KITC9_fkOPTaNCg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba2-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Of all the contractors I've hired ArmourGrade was by far the worst. Every time I walk outside I seem to find another problem. We had them replace gutters and soffit, here are just some of problems:

- Gutter leaks (they've attempted to fix one leak 3 times, after their third attempt I just fixed it myself).

- They don't know how to merge two pieces of j-channel together. Every joint around our entire house and garage is just two pieces overlapped, it looks absolutely horrible.

- Putting up soffit in a straight line over a 25 ft span? Not these guys. I cringe every time I look down the length of my garage.

- There are holes and gaps all over the place where animals can get into our roof.

- Water is getting down behind the aluminum facia they installed. During the last storm it was actually shooting a 4 ft stream of water out of the corner of the facia straight onto our deck. I've been told that shingle overhang was out of the scope of work. Here's my favourite bullet point from ArmourGrade's brochure when they sold me on the job: Inspect and ensure sufficient shingle overhang and drip edge.

- They installed a single piece of drip edge without asking me. The workers on site told me that they did it because they didn't think the shingles overhung the gutters enough. Right, like they are supposed to do (see above). A few days later they tacked on $168 to my final bill for it. A 10ft piece of drip edge costs less than $14. All they did was shove it under the shingles and walk away. In later discussion, I was told they actually installed 28 ft of drip edge. Their final invoice included drip edge installation, unit count 1, $168. I found the first 10ft of that 28ft long piece of drip edge, still not sure where the other 18ft went.

- Update, they came back weeks ago to fix more leaks, and put more drip edge up under the roof edge. They used MY drip edge, that I paid for. They actually said about the product I bought for a 15thof what they charged me "oh, this is better than what we use". Hilariously, they also said the drip edge that had previously been installed was not done properly. They were two of the same guys who had done the initial work. Said they'd come back to fix facia that overlaps the wrong way... it still overlaps the wrong way.

- When putting in a vent for a bathroom fan in the soffit they completely shredded the ducting on roofing nails. I didn't bother calling them about this one, I had to pull my fan out of the ceiling and rerun all the venting myself with the soffit already installed.

- I asked them to install black gutters and for all the drain tile caps at the bottom of the downspouts they installed white caps spray painted black on site. I asked them to get me black caps and they said this is what they always do and that they couldn't get black caps. I told them the paint would just come off and sure enough in a few months I had speckled white and black caps. I contacted their manager who actually told me that I should use better paint... what? This is a gutter company that can't get black drain caps, claims spray paint on plastic is an industry standard and when called out for it tells the customer to paint them again but with better paint than they used.

Anyone who has given ArmourGrade 5 stars needs to go outside and actually look at their workmanship.

I asked for a partial refund including compensation for the time I had to spend fixing their mistakes at the same rate I paid for additional labour on the job (either $80 or $100/hr depending on which employee you ask), and was offered $50/hr. When I said this was pretty insulting I was told that $50/hr is what they would pay a sub contractor, and aligns with industry standards. Should we do the math?

Hypothetically, let's say ArmourGrade sends someone to do 4 hrs of work on my house at $80/hr, and then I have to spend 2 hrs fixing their work and get reimbursed based on their ‘industry standard’:

-	The labourer makes $200
-	ArmourGrade makes $20 ($120 profit - $100 back to me)
-	I pay $220 and waste two hours of my time ($320 - $100)

Huh?","Images":["www.google.com/local/imagery/report/?cb_client=maps_sv.tactile&image_key=!1e10!2sCIHM0ogKEICAgIC7-Ie79AE&fid=0x0:0xa36e2532d2fb2615"],"When":"2024-8-12"},{"Name":"Noel Gopsill","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKBiDQr-uG2607PW5rD-NQdd3qWEpan10O-HRd-mThXie4JaQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"A very good experience with this company, the crew started at 7am and worked until 9:30pm to get the job done.
